Wasow §X.3 theorems (i) Newton-polygon slope-p/q to rank-q irregular singularity, (ii) characteristic exponents = roots of degree-q polynomial, (iii) Borel-singularity radius = 1/|c_root| cannot be supported by AEAL evidence at this dispatch.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (Birkhoff §2): birkhoff_1930.pdf has machine-readable text layer. pypdf 6.x extract_text succeeds across all 42 pages. §2 ('Statement of the Formal Problem') spans paper pp.209-214 (text-dump pp.6-10).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i): Birkhoff 1930 §2 formal-series existence theorem extracted at p.214 (verbatim quote, 28 words): 'It is our primary purpose in Part I of this paper [...] by proving that there always exist precisely n formal series solutions of the three types (6), (6'), (6").' States existence uniformly in equation order n.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i): Birkhoff §2 Newton-polygon construction at p.211 (verbatim quote, 28 words): 'a unique broken line L, concave upwards, whose vertices fall at certain of these points, while all of the other points lie above or on this line L'. Construction is uniform in n (no cap on number of vertices k <= n).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i): Birkhoff §2 'basic integer' p (Puiseux denominator) at p.209 (verbatim quote, 24 words): 'all possible values of p are evidently positive integral multiples of a least value p_0 >= 1'. Statement is uniform in p (no cap).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (i) verdict: C_BIRKHOFF_UNIFORM. Birkhoff 1930 §2 formal-series existence theorem applies uniformly in equation order n >= 1 and Puiseux denominator p >= 1. Specialised to PCF-1/PCF-2 (n = d, p = 1), this yields a uniform-in-d theorem-grade citation for the formal-direction half of PCF-2 v1.3 §3.3.A* (the xi_0(d) = d/beta_d^{1/d} claim).

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 (ii) verdict: C_BIRKHOFF_BOREL_NOT_IN_§§2-3. The Borel-transform leading-singularity claim (Prompt 018 §2 step 5 second bullet) is NOT in Birkhoff 1930 §§2-3. The word 'Borel' does not appear in the §§2-3 text dump. The relevant content lives elsewhere in the literature (likely Birkhoff-Trjitzinsky 1933 or Wasow §X.3); spec error noted in unexpected_finds.json.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.2 §3 result: uniqueness of converse correspondence (formal series -> difference equation), p.214 (verbatim quote, 28 words): 'It is an easy matter to solve the converse problem by demonstrating that to every such set of n (linearly independent) formal series there corresponds a uniquely determined linear difference equation of the n-th order.' Uniform in n.

Q20A dispatch 3 Phase C.3 verdict: C_LITERATURE_BLOCKED_AT_C1. Aggregate proof d-range = min(d_W*, d_B*, ∞) = undetermined because d_W* is unknown (Wasow blocked) and the Borel-half of d_B* is unknown (content not in Birkhoff §§2-3). Formal-half d-range from Birkhoff §2 is uniform (= ∞).
